destinations.evidence: Scheduled async parent context uses checked frequency*index, checked start+offset, and checked -1 for zero-based execution index semantics.
Async commitment compact codec stores origin_tx, origin_tx_rlp, ttl, and input metadata.
destinations.snippet: origin_tx: self.origin_tx, precompile_input: self.precompile_input.to_vec(), ttl: self.ttl, ... origin_tx_rlp: self.origin_tx_rlp.to_vec()

destinations.evidence: Scheduled compact codec preserves origin_tx, initial_block, frequency, ttl, and caller metadata.
destinations.snippet: origin_tx: self.origin_tx, call_id: self.call_id, index: self.index, initial_block: self.initial_block, frequency: self.frequency, ttl: self.ttl, caller: self.caller

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: scheduled async context uses checked zero-based block math and metadata codecs expose origin/ttl fields.
rationale: The December 5 API-refactor candidates supersede the earlier index+1 formula while retaining checked overflow handling. The metadata naming candidate remains compatible with the current origin_tx/origin_tx_rlp fields.
statement: For scheduled-origin async transactions, async detection and commitment validation compute simulation/commit block as startBlock + frequency * executionIndex - 1 using zero-based executionIndex and checked arithmetic; overflow returns ArithmeticOverflow, and async/scheduled metadata is exposed through origin_tx or origin_tx_rlp plus ttl naming.
suggested_falsification_tests: Use scheduled async tx with index 0 and assert context block is initial_block-1; use overflow frequency/index and assert error.
summary: Scheduled async simulation uses checked zero-based block math.
title: Scheduled async simulation uses zero-based checked block math
